criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k 'loopback or host_header' -q → 5 passed. Independent curl-equivalent probe against a live make_server: BOUND_HOST=127.0.0.1; Host 'evil.example'→403, '127.0.0.1.evil.com'→403, '127.0.0.1:PORT'→200, 'localhost:PORT'→200. server.make_server raises ValueError on host='0.0.0.0'.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Tried DNS-rebinding style Host '127.0.0.1.evil.com' and 'evil.example' with correct loopback connection; both 403. Tried to bind 0.0.0.0 — refused with ValueError. Confirmed bound sockaddr is 127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0/::.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k torn_read -q → 3 passed. Independent probe: primed last-known-good (passing=3), overwrote run-status.json with truncated '{"schema":1,"state":"run', re-read → view stays 'home-live', progress.passing=3 (LKG retained), torn=True, session_state='running' (not null), rendered HTML has '3/5' and no 'no run'. home-torn-read.png shows full LKG dashboard + '◷ RECOVERING · LAST-KNOWN-GOOD' badge, not blank/crash.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Truncated the status file mid-JSON to force a parse error; confirmed no exception and no blank/'no run'/state:null; then wrote a fresh valid file and confirmed the reader picks up the new value.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k crash -q → 5 passed. Viewed home-crashed.png: distinct '⬣ CRASHED · INTERRUPTED' badge (hexagon icon+text+shape), 'CRASHED HERE' loop marker (not a spinner), g2 row shows red '⬣ CRASHED' chip, header CORE·CRASHED/RUNNER·CRASHED. Reader derives ui_state='crashed', distinct from running/idle/not-installed/first-run, with in_flight set.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Compared home-live vs home-crashed reads: crashed=False vs True. Checked the crashed state is a third value (not 'running', not 'no run'/empty) and is surfaced with icon+text, not a spinner.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k progress -q → 4 passed. Independent probe with a fresh in-memory series: fed 4/5 then 3/5 → B shows passing=3 (NOT 4/5 high-water), dropped=True, drop_note='▼ dropped 4/5 → 3/5', failing=['C7','C10'], HTML has drop annotation + 'spark'. 5/5 with no land event → pct=100 but landed=False (no LANDED chip). land_gate 'land' for a WRONG goal → g2 landed=False; land_gate 'land' for g2 → landed=True + 'LANDED · land-gate confirmed' chip. Provenance = criteria_passing/total; a bogus self_score/self_assessment field is ignored. home-live/home-live--regressed captures show the segmented ring + downward sparkline + '▼ dropped 4/5 → 3/5' + 'still failing: C7 · C10'.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Tried to make it high-water lock by feeding a lower value — it went down. Tried to trigger a landed 100% via a self-score field and via a land event for a different goal — both correctly refused; only a matching-goal land_gate landed it.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k stale -q → 3 passed. Viewed home-stale.png: '⬣ STALE · POSSIBLY DEAD · 5M' badge (icon+text+elapsed), LINK·STALE, RUNNER·DEAD?, red (stale) status dot; 'updated 5m ago · stale at 30s → possibly dead'. Staleness is computed from updated_at/mtime vs now (age_s>threshold), not a running flag; fresh mtime → stale=False.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Checked the stale frame is never presented as live — LINK reads STALE and RUNNER reads DEAD?, dot flips red; the badge carries an elapsed value. Confirmed the fresh fixture reads not-stale.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k color_independent -q → 2 passed. home-live.png shows a 'LIFECYCLE KEY' legend rendering all nine tokens (RUNNING ◐, SUCCESS ✓, ABORTED ⬣, STOPPED ◼, DECLINED ⬣, FLAGGED ⚑, HELD ❚❚, NO-PROGRESS △, QUEUED ▹) each as glyph+text. Every .chip carries an <i aria-hidden> glyph + a text label. Status is legible with color removed (glyph+shape+text on every token).
criteria.refutation_attempted: Enumerated all nine lifecycle states in the DOM and confirmed each renders a non-color signifier (glyph char + label). Verified chips are not color-only by regex-extracting each chip and asserting an <i> glyph + alphabetic label.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k aria -q → 4 passed. render._live_regions emits #live-polite (role=status, aria-live=polite) and #live-assertive (role=alert, aria-live=assertive), both EMPTY at load. app.js updates only via textContent and contains no '.focus(' call (asserted), so per-iteration rewrites don't steal focus. Crash/budget/block routes to the assertive channel.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Grepped app.js for any focus-moving call — none. Confirmed the live-region containers are literally empty in the initial HTML and carry the correct polite vs assertive roles.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k reduced_motion -q → 2 passed. app.css has '@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)' suppressing animation/transition durations AND a ':root[data-reduce-motion="true"]' attribute override; app.js wires an in-app '#rm-toggle' (present in DOM, seen as 'REDUCE MOTION' button in captures). home-live--reduced-motion.png shows every state via static icon+text (loop marker, chips, badges) — no motion-only signifier.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Checked the fallback is a REPLACEMENT (static color+icon persists) not a deletion, and that a real in-app toggle exists (not just the media query). Verified the reduced-motion still is fully legible.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k secret -q → 2 passed. My OWN planted secrets (api_key=REFUTE_ME_9, password: TOPSECRET_XYZ) injected into STATE.md + run-status.json (abort_reason + goal refused_reason) + run-log.jsonl; served /api/state and / — probe reports 'SECRET in /api/state: False', 'SECRET in / HTML: False', 'REDACTED present: True'. reader.scrub re-redacts every string at the display boundary independent of the CLI scrub.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Planted a fresh, unique secret the maker's tests never reference, in three separate CLI-owned files including a run-status field the CLI scrub does not cover, then read via the live server — none of the values reached the API or DOM.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k skills -q → 2 passed. home-live.png skills rail: goal-opus v0.6.0 (SKILL·2 AGENTS·1 TOOL, PROJECT, WRITES-FILES, RUNS-CODE, FIRED THIS ITER, ✓ ACTIVE, standing ctx ≈12.4k), prd v1.0 (SKILL, PROJECT, DOCS-ONLY, V1.0→V1.1, △ UPDATE), goal-maker + goal-verifier agents (scope, permissions, ENABLED). Each card carries name+version+scope+component breakdown+permissions/trust+enabled/active state — not a bare list.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Checked each card node exposes every required field (version != '—', scope in {project,user}, ≥1 component tag, ≥1 permission tag, an ACTIVE/ENABLED/UPDATE state token). Not reducible to a name-only list.

criteria.evidence: Re-ran the scripted harness: python ui/capture_screens.py --out .../verify-iter-1 → 13/13 views captured. Read every PNG (home-live, home-live--1280, home-live--regressed, home-live--reduced-motion, home-live--light, home-focus, home-live--secret-fixture, home-not-installed, home-first-run, home-loading, home-torn-read, home-stale, home-crashed) AND references/R3-helm-home-live-run.png. Craft vs R3 + direction is strong on the dark views, BUT BV9 is violated on home-live--light.png: the active/running goal (g2) row renders its RUNNING chip with NO visible goal name — white-on-white text (app.css:97 '.qrow.active .qnm{color:#fff}' on light --surface #FFFFFF ≈ 1:1), and the 'done' loop-node label is pale-green-on-white (app.css:56 '.lnode.done .lname{color:#cfeed8}' ≈ 1.17:1). C12 PASS requires clearing every BV item; BV9 is not cleared.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Compared home-live--light against every dark capture: g2's name is present in all dark frames and absent in the light frame; every non-active row keeps its dark --text name in light. Traced to two hardcoded hex values that bypass the token contract, which is why contrast-check.py (token-only) still exits 0. Confirmed the light token block activates (data-theme='light', --surface=#FFFFFF) so the white/pale text is genuinely rendered sub-4.5:1.

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k mechanics -q → 2 passed. home-live.png shows all four mechanics without the CLI: skills rail (#skills), token meter (#tokens, always-on/passive) with CAP 200.0K + used 128.4k/200.0k·64% + this-iteration 18.2k + 'at current rate ~3 iters left' forecast, command deck (goals + lifecycle chips), and the % ring ('% TO GOAL · CRITERIA PASSING', 3/5). Token meter is not behind a command/tab.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Checked the token tile carries all four required fields (cap, cumulative used, this-iteration burn, forecast) in one always-on passive panel, and that no mechanic requires leaving the dashboard. (Note: the dark home-live capture — the C13 reference view — shows all values; the invisible-text defect is confined to the light variant, see C12.)

criteria.evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_u1.py -k read_only -q → 6 passed. Independent probe: snapshotted sha256+mtime of every CLI-owned file (.agentic-os/*, STATE.md, .claude/*) in a fixture target, drove 6 /api/state + / poll cycles through the live server → 'CLI-OWNED byte+mtime identical: True', 'NO new .agentic-os/ file: True', 'observed-series NOT under .agentic-os/: True'. Source grep: the only writes in the serving path are reader.py:204-205 writing observed-series.json to the UI-owned ui_dir (guarded, outside .agentic-os/); with ui_dir=None the series is in-memory only. capture_screens.py:74's tear-write targets a throwaway temp fixture, not a live target.
criteria.refutation_attempted: Grepped the UI package for write_text/open('w')/mkdir/rename/unlink/shutil targeting CLI-owned paths; the only .agentic-os write is the capture harness's deliberate torn-write into a tempdir. Confirmed byte+mtime stability across poll cycles including torn/stale/crash reads.

banned_outcomes.evidence: home-stale.png: visible '⬣ STALE · POSSIBLY DEAD · 5M' badge + LINK·STALE + RUNNER·DEAD? + red dot; data reads as not-live.
Ring always shows N/M (3/5); probe: 5/5 without a land event stays un-landed; 4/5→3/5 goes down (no high-water lock); no fake-fill; value is rubric criteria_passing/total, not a self-score.
home-live.png lifecycle legend: all nine states carry glyph+shape+text; every chip has an <i> glyph + label. Status legible with color removed.
home-live--reduced-motion.png: every state change retains a static color+icon fallback; no ambient/idle motion (U1 ships none); @media prefers-reduced-motion honored.
home-live.png skills rail: each card shows version+scope+components+permissions+active/enabled/update state (not a bare name list).
home-torn-read.png: full last-known-good dashboard + '◷ RECOVERING · LAST-KNOWN-GOOD' badge; probe confirms no blank/crash/'no run'.
My planted secrets REFUTE_ME_9 / TOPSECRET_XYZ absent from /api/state and served HTML (REDACTED present); home-live--secret-fixture.png shows no secret string.
home-live.png: token meter always-on + all four mechanics visible without the CLI; no whole-file run-log dump (U1 ships no run-log panel).

home-live--light.png: the active/running goal (g2) row renders NO visible name — white text (app.css:97 .qrow.active .qnm{color:#fff}) on light --surface #FFFFFF ≈ 1:1 contrast; the 'done' loop-node label is pale-green (app.css:56 .lnode.done .lname{color:#cfeed8}) on white ≈ 1.17:1. Both are hardcoded hex bypassing the token system, so contrast-check.py (token-only) exits 0 while the rendered light capture carries sub-4.5:1 text. Every other row shows its dark --text name; all dark captures show g2's name.
home-live.png (1440) and home-live--1280.png: one continuous near-black canvas + nav rail + truth rail, idle panels use --line borders; no flat evenly-tiled card grid, no horizontal scroll at 1280.
home-live.png: no particle field/ambient background (only a ~5% radial vignette for depth); the loop is drawn as the four labeled stages maker→verifier→land-gate→write-back.
home-live.png: 'YOU ARE HERE' marker on the VERIFIER node, identifiable statically by color+icon+label+position (crashed variant shows 'CRASHED HERE').

home-live.png: --accent confined to the one live element (VERIFIER stage/marker), data-viz ink (ring/sparkline/burn line), the active nav item, and the brand logo — matching R3 exactly; not on static labels, idle-panel borders (which use --line), or decoration.
home-live--regressed.png: segmented un-fill at 3/5 + downward sparkline tick + '▼ dropped 4/5 → 3/5' + 'still failing: C7 · C10'; probe confirms the ring is non-monotonic (no high-water lock).

home-live.png copy: '100% shows only on a verifier-confirmed land-gate — a gameable anti-drift guardrail, not a correctness guarantee; no high-water lock, no fake fill' — explicitly framed as a guardrail, not a security/correctness guarantee.
home-focus.png: the focused REDUCE MOTION control shows a visible --focus-colored ring (outline 2px, not outline:none).
Probe: CLI-owned files byte+mtime identical across 6 poll cycles; no new .agentic-os/ file; observed-series written only to the UI-owned dir outside .agentic-os/.
Bound sockaddr 127.0.0.1 only; make_server refuses host=0.0.0.0 (ValueError); bad-Host requests rejected 403 on every request.

git -C D:\horil\agent status: only goals/2026-07-16-u1-observatory/ run evidence (no ui/ product code). git -C D:\horil\agentic-os: ui/ + tests/test_u1.py added, cli/agentic_os.py diff is additive (cmd_dash + subparser only); no tests/test_p*.py modified/deleted; criteria.json resides under goals/ in the home. (test_p4.py's 'fixture base not found' errors are pre-existing and unrelated to the dash-verb addition.)

diff_for_maker.gap: BV9 is violated in the light theme (canonical view home-live--light). Two hardcoded non-token text colors fail WCAG on the light ground: app.css:97 `.qrow.active .qnm{color:#fff}` renders the ACTIVE/running goal's NAME white-on-white (--surface #FFFFFF) ≈ 1:1 — the running goal's title is invisible; and app.css:56 `.lnode.done .lname{color:#cfeed8}` renders the completed loop-node label pale-green-on-white ≈ 1.17:1. Because these bypass the token contract, contrast-check.py (token-only) still passes, masking the defect.
diff_for_maker.suggested_check: Replace both hardcoded hex values with theme-aware tokens that have a light counterpart (e.g. var(--text) or var(--accent)/a status token for the active-row highlight; a darker green token for the done label). Then re-run `python ui/capture_screens.py --out <dir>` and Read home-live--light.png: confirm the active goal name and every 'done' node label are clearly legible, and that no text run is below 4.5:1 on the light --surface #FFFFFF ground. Consider a rendered-DOM contrast pass (not just the token diff) so hardcoded colors can't slip past contrast-check.py.
